text.tspan: Two paths into the product, both ending in a clear next step.
Flow A is the priority path. Assessment CTA, symptom-first triage that branches into one of three Q2 sets, results, personalized recommendations. Flow B is the discovery path. Homepage,
Guides, article, related reviews or routines. Both surface a concrete next action; both feed the same recommendation surfaces.
For a man arriving with a specific symptom. Triage routes him into the right Q2 set, then to recommendations that map onto Guides, Routines, and Reviews.

Step 0 routes by location first because symptom-first beats nav-first for this audience. Q2 sets keep each branch under 4 questions. Completion holds above 70% in v2 testing. Amazon outbound is the v1 ending; the internal route is the pivot once products are live.
For a man arriving without a specific symptom. He browses Guides, lands in an article, and is offered related Reviews or Routines as the next action.
symptom tag rail filters this index

Article ends with two next actions, never one. A curated Review and a non-affiliate Routine so the path does not feel like an ad funnel. Symptom tags on the article and the index keep the rail consistent with Flow A.
